pubmed_links: [Garaffo et al. (2013) ST8SIA6 as a Dlx5 target in olfactory/GnRH neurodevelopment](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24427155/); [Huang et al. (2023) ST8SIA6 downregulated in dopaminergic neurons in a Parkinson's disease mouse model](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36977454/)
summary: ST8SIA6 encodes an alpha-2,8-sialyltransferase expressed in the developing olfactory/GnRH system, where knockdown in zebrafish disrupts olfactory axon extension and GnRH neuron migration relevant to Kallmann syndrome; it is also neuroprotective in dopaminergic neurons in a Parkinson's disease model. No direct evidence links ST8SIA6 to autism, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, or intellectual disability, though its close paralog ST8SIA2 is a well-established schizophrenia gene. (novel candidate)
